title: Lethedon ciliaris
intro: Lethedon ciliaris, also known as Sweet Woodruff or Galium odoratum, is a low-growing, perennial herb belonging to the family Rubiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to aid in digestion, relieve anxiety, and reduce inflammation, making it a popular herbal remedy for stomach issues and stress relief. The bioactive constituents of Lethedon ciliaris include coumarins, flavonoids, and volatile oils, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Sweet Woodruff include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation, soothe digestive issues, and support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Lethedon ciliaris is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. The Celastraceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the staff vines or bittersweet. Lethedon ciliaris is native to the tropical regions of Asia. It is a type of deciduous shrub or small tree. The plant's classification is further supported by its presence in various botanical classifications and databases.
uses: Lethedon ciliaris is known to have various medicinal uses, primarily in traditional medicine. It is traditionally used to treat skin conditions, such as wounds and eczema, due to its anti-inflammatory properties. The plant also has antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating infections. Additionally, it is used to treat respiratory issues, including coughs and colds. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of its medicinal uses.

properties: Lethedon ciliaris, also known as Indian spurge or spurge, has been used in traditional medicine for its therapeutic properties. The plant contains various bioactive compounds that have been studied for their potential health benefits.
